tasks.title: Create and Optimize Pylint Configuration File
tasks.description: Generate a .pylintrc file tailored to the project's codebase, ensuring alignment with Python best practices and maintainability goals.
tasks.details:
1. Use the 'pylint --generate-rcfile' command to create a baseline .pylintrc file in the project root.
2. Analyze the project's current coding standards, frameworks, and directory structure.
3. Enable recommended checkers and disable irrelevant or conflicting ones, such as those based on project-specific formatting tools (e.g., Black or isort).
4. Configure message control to suppress noise and focus on meaningful issues (e.g., enabling error and warning messages but silencing style checks covered by other tools).
5. Customize naming conventions, docstring requirements, and import rules in the config to match team and project requirements.
6. Add configuration for plugins (such as pylint-django or pylint-flask) if using major frameworks.
7. Document key decisions and where the configuration differs from default Pylint recommendations. Accompany the file with brief documentation in the README or a dedicated doc file.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run 'pylint' against representative modules and ensure the tool executes successfully with the new configuration.
2. Confirm that expected style, import, and docstring violations are flagged, and suppressed messages are not reported.
3. Verify that enabling or disabling rules aligns with team and project requirements by reviewing and, if possible, approving with the team.
4. Integrate Pylint with CI/CD (if present) to confirm consistent use of the configuration across contributors.
5. Optionally, adjust based on initial team feedback and code reviews, then re-run tests to ensure stability.

tasks.title: Test task from script
tasks.description: A task created by test_call_tool_add_task.py to verify the add_task tool works correctly.

tasks.title: Test task from script
tasks.description: A task created by test_call_tool_add_task.py to verify the add_task tool works correctly.

tasks.title: Generate Initial Pylint Configuration File
tasks.description: Create a baseline .pylintrc configuration file for the project, ensuring it aligns with Python best practices and supports maintainable code.
tasks.details: Use the command 'pylint --generate-rcfile' to generate a default .pylintrc file and place it in the project root directory. Review the generated configuration and adjust it based on the project's Python version, coding standards, and folder structure. Consult with the team to identify any specific frameworks, libraries, or conventions in use that might require enabling or disabling certain Pylint checkers. Ensure compatibility with other tooling (e.g., Black, isort) by disabling overlapping checks to prevent redundant warnings. Document any customizations in the configuration for future maintainers. Commit the .pylintrc file to version control.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Run 'pylint' against a selection of files to verify the linter executes without configuration errors. 2. Confirm that expected errors and warnings are reported, and that suppressed messages are not displayed. 3. Validate compatibility with other code quality tools such as Black or isort. 4. Seek feedback from the team to ensure the generated configuration meets their expectations and doesn't inhibit common workflows.

tasks.title: Finalize and Document Pylint Configuration File
tasks.description: Review, finalize, and document the .pylintrc configuration for the project, ensuring it is clearly explained and ready for team adoption.
tasks.details:
1. Examine the baseline .pylintrc file created in the previous task and adjust settings for clarity and maintainability.
2. Add detailed inline comments within the .pylintrc file to explain key rule choices, disabled or enabled checkers, and any project-specific customizations.
3. Create or update accompanying documentation (e.g., README section or a docs/code_quality.md file) to summarize how the team should use Pylint, the rationale for configuration decisions, and integration instructions with other tools (such as Black or isort).
4. Coordinate with the development team to gather feedback and confirm the configuration supports current workflows and coding standards.
5. Ensure that all configuration decisions are justified and reproducible for new team members.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Manually review the .pylintrc file to verify all adjustments are properly documented and justified.
2. Review additional documentation to confirm instructions are clear and up to date.
3. Run Pylint against representative code files using the finalized configuration to ensure no unexpected errors or suppressed rules are present.
4. Solicit a review from at least one other team member to validate readability and understanding of both the config and associated documentation.

tasks.title: Customize Pylint Configuration for Project Standards
tasks.description: Modify the generated .pylintrc file to align precisely with the team's Python standards, coding conventions, and tooling requirements.
tasks.details:
1. Review the baseline .pylintrc file created in the previous task and analyze its default settings in the context of the project's codebase.
2. Meet with stakeholders or review documentation to identify any team-specific standards, frameworks (e.g., Django, Flask), or special library usages that require adjusting Pylint settings.
3. Enable or disable specific checkers to reduce noise and enhance actionable feedback, especially where rules might conflict with tools like Black or isort.
4. Adjust thresholds and configuration values (e.g., max-line-length, variable naming conventions, ignored-patterns) to fit the team's needs.
5. Annotate key sections of the .pylintrc with inline comments explaining the rationale behind any non-default decisions.
6. Save and commit the updated .pylintrc, ensuring it is placed at the project root and is easy for other contributors to locate and extend in the future.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run 'pylint' on a representative sample of project files with the customized .pylintrc, verifying that checks execute correctly and only meaningful messages appear.
2. Check for absence of configuration errors and ensure there are no unexpected warnings or failures due to custom settings.
3. Validate that disabled rules (e.g., formatting issues handled by Black) are not reported and that project-specific issues (e.g., naming conventions) are enforced.
4. Solicit feedback from team members on the clarity and effectiveness of the new configuration.
5. Document passing and failing examples to ensure the configuration delivers intended results.

tasks.title: Create Pylint Configuration File Tailored for the Project
tasks.description: Develop a .pylintrc configuration file for the project by generating, refining, and customizing it to align with the team's coding standards, Python version, and tooling preferences.
tasks.details:
1. Use the command `pylint --generate-rcfile` to generate a default .pylintrc file in the project’s root directory.
2. Thoroughly review the generated configuration for compatibility with the project’s Python version, frameworks (such as Django, Flask, etc.), and directory structure.
3. Collaborate with team members or refer to the project's style guidelines to identify rules and checkers to enable or disable, especially in relation to other formatting or linter tools like Black and isort.
4. Adjust message control settings to suppress irrelevant warnings and errors, focusing on actionable issues.
5. Include modifications to cater for commonly used third-party libraries, custom plugins, or code patterns prevalent in the codebase.
6. Document key rule changes with comments in the .pylintrc file to aid maintainability and facilitate team understanding.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run 'pylint' with the new configuration file on a representative sample of project files to ensure it executes without errors or unexpected warnings.
2. Confirm that critical expected violations (such as errors or warnings relevant to the team's standards) are flagged, while non-pertinent messages are suppressed.
3. Verify that the configuration does not conflict with other code quality tools (run Black and isort where applicable in combination with Pylint and check results).
4. Solicit feedback from at least one other team member on the effectiveness and clarity of the configuration.
5. Make targeted adjustments based on initial test and feedback, rerunning the above checks until all acceptance criteria are met.

tasks.title: Create and Commit Pylint Config File for Project
tasks.description: Generate and commit the project's .pylintrc file using the recommended toolchain and team conventions, incorporating feedback from previous configuration and customization tasks.
tasks.details:
1. Use 'pylint --generate-rcfile' to create an initial .pylintrc file in the project's root directory if not already present. 2. Review existing Pylint-related tasks and integrate the finalized or customized configurations from past iterations (especially those resulting from team discussions, customization, or documentation steps).
3. Merge any key rule adjustments, suppression lists, or formatting integrations (e.g., with Black, isort) into the .pylintrc, ensuring consistency with documented decisions.
4. Commit the finalized file to version control, following repository standards for documenting configuration changes in commit messages (e.g., reference task numbers, summarize key customizations).
5. Coordinate with the team if further feedback is needed before the file is marked as the official linter configuration.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Validate that the committed .pylintrc file is present in the project root and readable by running 'pylint' on sample modules or packages, ensuring there are no file not found or syntax errors. 2. Confirm the configuration enforces only the intended rules by explicitly triggering test errors (e.g., import style violation or missing docstring) and ensuring the correct checks are reported or suppressed. 3. Cross-check that the configuration integrates appropriately with other code quality tools (run Black, isort, and Pylint in sequence). 4. Review git history to ensure changes are clearly documented and reference related tasks. 5. Solicit feedback from a team member or reviewer to verify that all necessary customizations have been captured.

tasks.title: Implement and Integrate Project-Wide Pylint Configuration File
tasks.description: Create, refine, and finalize a .pylintrc file tailored to the project's requirements, ensuring alignment with team standards, supported tooling, and prior configuration efforts.
tasks.details: 1. Review previous iterations of .pylintrc files and team feedback regarding rule customizations, compatibility with tools like Black and isort, and documented conventions. 2. Aggregate final configuration options from discussions, documentation (including changes from earlier customization and documentation tasks), and team-wide requirements. 3. Use `pylint --generate-rcfile` to regenerate a default file if substantial changes are needed, then merge in prior customizations. 4. Update or annotate the .pylintrc file with clear comments explaining enabled/disabled checkers and relevant integrations. 5. Ensure compatibility with project structure, Python version, and any frameworks in use. 6. Commit the finalized .pylintrc file to version control at the project root, replacing previous configs as necessary. 7. Communicate updates to all team members and update documentation to reflect current configuration and usage instructions.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Validate that the finalized .pylintrc file exists in the project root and is being used by default when running Pylint. 2. Run Pylint on a representative subset of modules/packages to ensure the linter operates without errors and enforces the intended ruleset. 3. Deliberately trigger errors/warnings that should and should not be reported, verifying configuration accuracy. 4. Check for harmonious operation alongside Black and isort, ensuring no conflicting style or import errors. 5. Confirm all comments and documentation in the config and supporting files are clear, up to date, and accessible to the team.

tasks.title: Create a Comprehensive Pylint Configuration File for the Project
tasks.description: Develop a finalized .pylintrc configuration file by consolidating previous configurations, team standards, and compatibility requirements with other tooling. Ensure that the file is committed and ready for immediate project-wide use.
tasks.details:
1. Gather all previously generated and customized .pylintrc files created in earlier configuration tasks, paying close attention to rule changes, suppression lists, and alignment with tools like Black and isort.
2. Review feedback from team discussions, task documentation, and coding standards to decide on final rule adjustments and checkers to be enabled or disabled.
3. Integrate key rule modifications, ensuring the configuration enforces the team's preferred linting quality while not conflicting with automated formatting tools.
4. Add/retain explanatory comments where necessary, clarifying any project-specific customizations or deviations from default behavior.
5. Commit the finalized .pylintrc file to the project root directory and, if applicable, update any related documentation (e.g., a code quality guide or integration notes in the README).
6. Announce the finalized configuration to the team and ensure all contributors are aware of and able to use the standardized Pylint workflow.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run 'pylint' using the committed .pylintrc file on multiple representative source files and modules to ensure there are no configuration or file not found errors.
2. Intentionally trigger warnings and errors (according to the enabled rules) in sample files, verifying that the linter flags violations as expected and suppresses messages per the configuration.
3. Validate that the configuration does not interfere with or contradict code formatting tools (e.g., Black, isort) by running them in sequence and confirming expected results.
4. Have a team member or reviewer verify the .pylintrc content and its placement, as well as confirm documentation accurately describes configuration usage.
